verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase, the canonical compartment. However, a well-documented minority pool localizes to the plasma membrane with the catalytic domain exposed on the cell surface, where it functions as a cell-surface lectin/adhesion molecule (e.g., in sperm-egg binding, neurite outgrowth, and cell-cell recognition). The same type II TM topology that anchors it in the Golgi anchors it at the PM with the ectodomain facing outward when trafficked there. This dual Golgi/PM localization is its own anchor mechanism, qualifying as dual_localization rather than no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and therapeutic tractability of the PM-resident pool relative to dominant Golgi localization.
